source_citation: Options Trading, Euan Sinclair, Ch.10, p.199-249
raw_chunk_text:
Another enhancement is to take advantage of the fact that selling
options will do better in stable or declining VIX environments. If we
only sell options when the VIX is below its moving average (here
rule_text: A trader should only sell options when the VIX is below its moving average, as this regime filter improves performance by avoiding high-volatility environments unfavorable to short option positions.
exit_conditions: Position max profit or loss target hit
hallucination_issues: MA period of 60 not specified in source - only decay factor 0.95 mentioned
Exit conditions not mentioned in excerpt - purely inferred
Second entry condition 'Options selling setup identified' is vague and not from source
haiku_reason: The entry condition specifies a quantifiable technical trigger (VIX < EWMA with defined parameters) that can be directly implemented in code, and the exit conditions provide clear, measurable criteria for position management.
